title: Major Incident Post-Mortem Compliance

compliance.assurance_rationale: APRA CPS 234 23 Incident management — post-mortem compliance is a direct evidence source for APRA's incident-management requirement.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that APRA CPS 234 23 (Incident management) is enforced — Splunk UC-16.1.20: Major Incident Post-Mortem Compliance.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-16.1.20' running on Incidents with major_incident or u_major_incident, post-mortem fields, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" u_major_incident=true state="closed" earliest=-90d
| eval pm_due=resolved_at + (7*86400)
| eval compliant=if(post_mortem_completed=true OR now() <= pm_due OR isnotnull(u_post_mortem_date),1,0)
| where post_mortem_completed=false AND now() > pm_due
| table number, short_description, resolved_at, pm_due, assignment_group
| sort resolved_at
description: Verifies that major incidents have completed post-mortems within policy — extending generic PIR tracking with explicit compliance scoring for Sev1 programs.
value: Verifies that major incidents have completed post-mortems within policy — extending generic PIR tracking with explicit compliance scoring for Sev1 programs.
implementation: Align field names with your form (`u_post_mortem_complete`, tasks, etc.). Use related task table if post-mortem is a child task. Weekly report of breaches; executive summary of compliance %.
visualization: Table (non-compliant MIs), Single value (compliance %), Line chart (compliance trend).
knownFalsePositives: Late completion when a major event is still open, legal review slows close-out, or a new runbook is not followed yet.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Incidents with `major_incident` or `u_major_incident`, post-mortem fields.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Align field names with your form (`u_post_mortem_complete`, tasks, etc.). Use related task table if post-mortem is a child task. Weekly report of breaches; executive summary of compliance %.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" u_major_incident=true state="closed" earliest=-90d
| eval pm_due=resolved_at + (7*86400)
| eval compliant=if(post_mortem_completed=true OR now() <= pm_due OR isnotnull(u_post_mortem_date),1,0)
| where post_mortem_completed=false AND now() > pm_due
| table number, short_description, resolved_at, pm_due, assignment_group
| sort resolved_at
```

Understanding this SPL

**Major Incident Post-Mortem Compliance** — Verifies that major incidents have completed post-mortems within policy — extending generic PIR tracking with explicit compliance scoring for Sev1 programs.

Documented **Data sources**: Incidents with `major_incident` or `u_major_incident`, post-mortem fields. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:incident", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pm_due** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **compliant** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where post_mortem_completed=false AND now() > pm_due` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Major Incident Post-Mortem Compliance**): table number, short_description, resolved_at, pm_due, assignment_group
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow list or form that matches this data (for example the incident, request, or problem view). Reconcile a few record numbers, states, and times. Confirm the add-on poll or export time lines up with what you see in the browser.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (non-compliant MIs), Single value (compliance %), Line chart (compliance trend).
grandmaExplanation: We track whether the big events get a learning review, so the organization actually improves after the all-hands fire drill.
